slides.title: Olga's Flute Vocabulary
slides.content:
Let's learn new words from Olga's story:

🎶 flute — a musical instrument you blow into
🎤 solo — playing or singing alone, by yourself
💧 wet — covered in water, not dry
😰 nervous — worried, a bit scared
🔌 cork — a small piece that blocks a hole
🥇 proud — feeling good about what you did
slides.tip: A flute = una flauta. Solo means doing something on your own!

slides.title: Olga's Big Concert
slides.html:
Olga plays the flute at school. She practises every day after lunch.

Today is the school concert! Olga has a solo — she has to play all by herself. She feels very nervous.

Oh no! Her hands are wet because she is so nervous. She drops the cork from her flute! But she picks it up quickly and starts to play.

The music is beautiful! Everyone claps. Olga feels so proud. She already wants to play again!

slides.title: Adverbs of Time
slides.content:
Adverbs of time tell us when something happens:

📅 every day — each day, again and again
✅ already — it happened before now
🌅 in the morning — the early part of the day
🌙 in the evening — the late part of the day

Example: I practise the flute every day. I have already finished my homework.
slides.tip: Adverbs of time answer the question: When?

slides.title: When Does It Happen?
slides.problem:
Choose the correct adverb of time:

1. I brush my teeth _____ (every day / already).
2. She has _____ (in the morning / already) eaten breakfast.
3. We play football _____ (in the evening / already).
4. He reads a story _____ (every day / in the morning) before bed.
steps: 1. Brushing teeth is a daily habit --> every day
2. She finished eating before now --> already
3. Playing after school in the late part of the day --> in the evening
4. Reading before bed is a daily habit --> every day
slides.answer: 1. every day  2. already  3. in the evening  4. every day

slides.title: Phrasal Verbs (Part 1)
slides.content:
A phrasal verb is two words that go together with a special meaning:

🍽️ have lunch — to eat the midday meal
🎵 give a concert — to perform music for an audience
❌ make a mistake — to do something wrong

Examples:
We have lunch at 1 o'clock.
The school band will give a concert on Friday.
Don't worry if you make a mistake!

slides.title: Phrasal Verbs (Part 2)
slides.content:
More phrasal verbs to learn:

🎨 do an art project — to work on a creative task
🤥 tell a lie — to say something that is not true

Examples:
We do an art project every Wednesday.
You must not tell a lie. Always tell the truth!
slides.tip: Be careful! We say MAKE a mistake, but DO a project. We say GIVE a concert, but TELL a lie.

slides.title: Choose the Right Verb
slides.problem:
Complete with have, give, make, do or tell:

1. We _____ lunch at school.
2. The band will _____ a concert.
3. Don't _____ a mistake!
4. We _____ an art project today.
5. You must not _____ a lie.
steps: 1. Lunch goes with HAVE --> have lunch
2. Concert goes with GIVE --> give a concert
3. Mistake goes with MAKE --> make a mistake
4. Art project goes with DO --> do an art project
5. Lie goes with TELL --> tell a lie

slides.title: A Busy Day
slides.html:
In the morning, Olga practises her flute. She plays her solo three times. She has already learnt all the notes!

At 12 o'clock, she goes to have lunch with her friends. After lunch, they do an art project — they paint pictures of musical instruments.

In the evening, the school band will give a concert. Olga feels a bit nervous, but she doesn't make a mistake! Her parents feel very proud.

slides.title: Common Mistakes
slides.content:
Be careful with these phrasal verbs! Students often mix them up:

✅ have lunch — NOT do lunch
✅ give a concert — NOT make a concert
✅ make a mistake — NOT do a mistake
✅ do a project — NOT make a project
✅ tell a lie — NOT say a lie
slides.tip: Try to learn each phrasal verb as one piece, like a puzzle that always goes together!

slides.title: Olga's Diary
slides.problem:
Fill in the gaps in Olga's diary entry:

Today I _____ a concert at school. I was very _____ but I didn't _____ any mistakes. My hands were _____ but I played my _____ perfectly. I feel so _____!
steps: Concert goes with GIVE --> gave a concert
Doing something wrong goes with MAKE --> make
slides.answer: gave, nervous, make, wet, solo, proud

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🎶 Vocabulary: flute, solo, wet, nervous, cork, proud
⏰ Adverbs of time: every day, already, in the morning, in the evening
🍽️ have lunch, 🎵 give a concert, ❌ make a mistake
🎨 do an art project, 🤥 tell a lie
⚠️ Each phrasal verb uses a specific verb — learn them together!
